---
title: "Power Analysis for Linear Mixed Effects Model"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-11"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
options(width = 100)
```

Clear environment
```{r, include=FALSE}
rm(list = ls(all.names = TRUE))
```

Install packages
```{r}
install.packages(c(
  "lme4",
  "simr",
  "tidyverse",
  "broom",
  "ggeffects"
))
```

Load libraries
```{r, include=FALSE}
library(lme4)
library(simr)
library(tidyverse)
library(broom)
library(ggeffects)
```

Load pilot data
```{r}

path  <- "~/labrotation.si@gmail.com - Google Drive/My Drive/Hasib Thesis/ExperimentDataConvert/dataAnalysis/dataFiles"

files <-list.files(path, pattern = "\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE, recursive = FALSE)
names(files) <- basename(files)

read_one <- function(f){
  df <- read_csv(f, show_col_types = FALSE,
                 na = c("", "NA", "NaN"),
                 locale = locale(encoding = "UTF-8"),
                 guess_max = 100000)
  if ("ID" %in% names(df)) mutate(df, ID = as.character(.data$ID)) else df
}

DT <- map_dfr(files, read_one, .id = "source_file") %>%
  relocate(source_file, .before = 1)

# Check structure
glimpse(DT)
```

Prepare data for LMM
```{r}

# Convert signal_detection to binary: Hit = 1, others = 0
DT <- DT %>%
  mutate(Hit_binary = ifelse(signal_detection == "Hit", 1, 0))

# Check the data
DT %>%
  group_by(ID, condition) %>%
  summarise(
    n_trials = n(),
    n_hits = sum(Hit_binary),
    hit_rate = mean(Hit_binary),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  head(20)

# Check unique conditions and gapOf values
cat("Unique conditions:", unique(DT$condition), "\n")
cat("Unique gapOf:", sort(unique(DT$gapOf)), "\n")

```

Fit base LMM
```{r}

# Fit the linear mixed effects model
# Formula: Hit_Rate ~ condition * gapOf + (1|ID)

# Option 1: Using binary outcome with binomial family
# model_binomial <- glmer(Hit_binary ~ condition * gapOf + (1|ID), 
#                         data = DT, family = binomial)

# Option 2: Using hit rate as continuous (requires aggregation)
# Calculate hit rate per participant per condition per gapOf
DT_agg <- DT %>%
  group_by(ID, condition, gapOf) %>%
  summarise(
    n_trials = n(),
    n_hits = sum(Hit_binary),
    hit_rate = mean(Hit_binary),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Fit LMM with aggregated data
model <- lmer(hit_rate ~ condition * gapOf + (1|ID), data = DT_agg)

summary(model)

# Check model diagnostics
plot(model)

```

Check fixed effects
```{r}

# Get fixed effects estimates
fixef(model)

# Confidence intervals
confint(model)

```

Power analysis using simr
```{r}

# Power for fixed effects
# Run simulation to test power for condition effect

# Test power for main effect of condition
power_condition <- powerSim(model, 
                            test = fixed("condition", "lr"),
                            nsim = 100,
                            seed = 42)

print(power_condition)

# Test power for interaction
power_interaction <- powerSim(model,
                              test = fixed("condition:gapOf", "lr"),
                              nsim = 100,
                              seed = 42)

print(power_interaction)

```

Power curve - vary number of subjects
```{r}

# Create power curve to find required sample size
# Extend the model with more subjects
model_ext <- extend(model, along = "ID", n = 50)

# Run power curve
pc <- powerCurve(model_ext,
                 test = fixed("condition", "lr"),
                 along = "ID",
                 nsim = 100,
                 seed = 42)

print(pc)
plot(pc)

```

Find minimum sample size for 80% power
```{r}

# Test different sample sizes to find minimum for 80% power
# Start with current sample size
current_n <- length(unique(DT_agg$ID))
cat("Current sample size:", current_n, "\n")

# Test increasing sample sizes
test_sizes <- c(20, 30, 40, 50, 60, 80, 100)

power_results <- data.frame(
  n_subjects = integer(),
  power = numeric()
)

for (n in test_sizes) {
  cat("Testing n =", n, "...\n")
  model_ext <- extend(model, along = "ID", n = n)
  p <- powerSim(model_ext, 
                test = fixed("condition", "lr"),
                nsim = 50,  # fewer simulations for speed
                seed = 42)
  power_results <- rbind(power_results, data.frame(n_subjects = n, power = p$p))
}

print(power_results)

# Plot power curve
ggplot(power_results, aes(x = n_subjects, y = power)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.80, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(x = "Number of Subjects", y = "Power", title = "Power Curve for Condition Effect") +
  theme_minimal()

# Find minimum n for 80% power
min_n <- power_results %>%
  filter(power >= 0.80) %>%
  slice_min(n_subjects) %>%
  pull(n_subjects)

if (is.na(min_n)) {
  cat("80% power not achieved within tested range. Consider larger sample sizes.\n")
} else {
  cat("Minimum sample size for 80% power:", min_n, "\n")
}

```

Power for gapOf effect
```{r}

# Test power for gapOf main effect
power_gapof <- powerSim(model,
                        test = fixed("gapOf", "lr"),
                        nsim = 100,
                        seed = 42)

print(power_gapof)

```

Summary
```{r}

cat("=== Power Analysis Summary ===\n\n")
cat("Model: hit_rate ~ condition * gapOf + (1|ID)\n\n")
cat("Current sample size:", current_n, "subjects\n\n")

cat("Power results:\n")
cat("- Condition effect power:", power_condition$p, "\n")
cat("- GapOf effect power:", power_gapof$p, "\n")
cat("- Interaction power:", power_interaction$p, "\n\n")

if (!is.na(min_n)) {
  cat("Minimum subjects needed for 80% power:", min_n, "\n")
}

cat("\nRecommendations:\n")
if (power_condition$p < 0.80) {
  cat("- Increase sample size to achieve 80% power for condition effect\n")
} else {
  cat("- Current sample size is sufficient for condition effect\n")
}

```

Save results
```{r}

# Save power results to CSV
write_csv(power_results, "power_analysis_results.csv")

# Save summary
sink("power_analysis_summary.txt")
cat("=== Power Analysis Summary ===\n\n")
cat("Model: hit_rate ~ condition * gapOf + (1|ID)\n\n")
cat("Current sample size:", current_n, "subjects\n\n")
cat("Power results:\n")
cat("- Condition effect power:", power_condition$p, "\n")
cat("- GapOf effect power:", power_gapof$p, "\n")
cat("- Interaction power:", power_interaction$p, "\n\n")
if (!is.na(min_n)) {
  cat("Minimum subjects needed for 80% power:", min_n, "\n")
}
sink()

cat("Results saved to power_analysis_results.csv and power_analysis_summary.txt\n")

```
